Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal Of Lawsuit Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Procedure 581
Off Calendar May-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF REGINA CORBIN'S, INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF OTHER INDIVIDUALS SIMILARLY SITUATED, Motion For Dismissal Of Lawsuit Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Procedure 581.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Kiley Lynn Grombacher, Esq., of Bradley/Grombacher, LLP, (805) 270-7100, for Plaintiff.
Tim Kolesk, Esq., of Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP, (213) 229-7000, for Defendant.

The matter is argued. At conclusion of argument the court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Regina Corbin's unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Lawsuit Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Procedure 581 is DENIED. The court will not authorize dismissal under Code of Civil Procedure section 581(b)(1) absent more information, in light of the fact that dismissal under this statute generally does not require a court order. The motion makes no showing why a court order is necessary or appropriate. Further, this is a class action and it appears the request for dismissal is based on a settlement. A settlement of a class action or class claims requires court approval. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.769.) Dismissal of a class action requires court approval. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.770.)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After hearing, the court signs an order.
